one_line_ask: Clean up and on-brand-tint three raw product-UI screenshots, vectorize the Nimbadesk logo, then run the client's authored one-page sell-sheet InDesign template through a data-merge against a product-tier CSV to produce a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG per tier.
brief: Nimbadesk, a B2B SaaS company that sells a customer-support help-desk platform, needs its product sell sheets finished for print and for the sales team's leave-behind kit. Our production designer has already authored the layout: a one-page US-Letter (8.5" x 11" portrait) sell-sheet InDesign template (nimbadesk_sellsheet.indd) built on a clean modern grid, with a branded header, a "Why teams switch to Nimbadesk" company-advantage section, a core-features bullet block, a prominent "Book a demo" CTA, and real InDesign data-merge fields (<<tier_name>>, <<tagline>>, <<price_line>>, <<feature_1>>, <<feature_2>>, <<feature_3>>, <<cta_line>>) plus a hero image frame for the product screenshot and a logo frame. This is a production/finishing job, NOT a fresh branding exercise. We will hand off: the authored .indd template, a brand kit (hex palette #0E1F3D deep navy + #3B82F6 signal blue + #F5F7FA fog-grey, typography notes), three raw product-UI screenshots (the ticket inbox, the analytics dashboard, the automation builder) that were captured on-screen and look dull, slightly tilted and cool/washed-out, the Nimbadesk logo as a flat PNG, and a CSV holding one row per product tier (Starter, G
